profiles.name: Arjun Sharma
profiles.bio: Building LLM-powered developer tools that help engineers ship 10x faster. Ex-Google Brain, IIT Bombay CSE. I believe the next decade will be defined by how well we pair human creativity with machine intelligence.
profiles.ikigai_love: Designing intelligent systems that learn from human feedback and improve autonomously over time
profiles.ikigai_good_at: Architecting scalable ML pipelines, fine-tuning LLMs for domain-specific tasks, and translating research papers into production systems
profiles.ikigai_world_needs: Developer tools that eliminate repetitive cognitive load so engineers can focus on solving meaningful problems
profiles.ikigai_paid_for: ML engineering, LLM product development, and technical consulting for AI-native startups
profiles.ikigai_mission: To build AI infrastructure that democratises software creation for the next billion developers

match_criteria.collaboration_style: I work best with a product-minded co-founder who can own GTM and customer discovery while I drive engineering and research. Bias toward async written communication with weekly syncs.
match_criteria.deal_breakers: No genuine conviction about the problem, unwilling to talk to users, or purely motivated by AI hype with no technical depth
match_criteria.summary: Ex-Google Brain ML engineer seeking a product-minded co-founder to build AI developer tooling. Strong engineering foundation in place — need someone who can own growth, fundraising, and customer discovery.

profiles.name: Priya Nair
profiles.bio: Doctor turned healthtech entrepreneur. Building remote diagnostics infrastructure for Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities in India. AIIMS Delhi alumna. Passionate about making quality healthcare accessible, not just affordable.
profiles.ikigai_love: Bridging the gap between world-class medical expertise and underserved communities through technology
profiles.ikigai_good_at: Clinical workflow design, healthcare regulatory navigation, and building trust with patients and providers in resource-constrained settings
profiles.ikigai_world_needs: Scalable remote diagnostics platforms that work reliably in low-bandwidth, offline-capable environments across rural India
profiles.ikigai_paid_for: Healthcare product consulting, clinical validation studies, and grant writing for health impact organisations
profiles.ikigai_mission: To ensure a child born in rural Bihar has access to the same diagnostic accuracy as one born in South Delhi

match_criteria.collaboration_style: Looking for a deeply mission-aligned technical co-founder comfortable spending time in the field. I value written documentation over long meetings and iterative product thinking over big upfront design.
match_criteria.deal_breakers: Purely profit-driven with no empathy for the end user, or unwilling to spend time understanding the clinical context
match_criteria.summary: AIIMS-trained doctor building remote diagnostics for Bharat. Seeking a technical co-founder with mobile and cloud expertise. Clinical validation and regulatory groundwork already done.

profiles.name: Rahul Gupta
profiles.bio: Fintech engineer with 6 years building payment infrastructure at Razorpay and PhonePe. Obsessed with real-time payment systems and financial inclusion. Now exploring what AI-native banking looks like for Gen Z India.
profiles.ikigai_love: Designing payment systems that just work — fast, secure, and invisible to the end user
profiles.ikigai_good_at: High-throughput distributed systems, UPI stack integrations, fraud detection pipelines, and backend API design at scale
profiles.ikigai_world_needs: Transparent, low-cost financial infrastructure that empowers individuals without an existing credit history or banking relationship
profiles.ikigai_paid_for: Backend engineering, payments architecture consulting, and system design for fintech startups
profiles.ikigai_mission: To build the financial OS for the next 500 million Indians entering the formal economy

match_criteria.collaboration_style: Prefer small high-conviction teams where I own the entire backend stack and collaborate closely with product. Daily standups, clear ownership, strong code review culture.
match_criteria.deal_breakers: Bureaucratic team structure, unclear technical direction, or founders who treat engineering as a pure cost centre
match_criteria.summary: Payments backend specialist with deep UPI and real-time systems experience. Seeking a mission-driven founding team for a fintech or AI-native banking product. Can own all backend infrastructure from day one.

profiles.name: Ananya Krishnan
profiles.bio: Former CBSE school teacher turned EdTech product builder. Spent 5 years in classrooms understanding how children actually learn, now building adaptive learning systems that meet students where they are. Based in Chennai.
profiles.ikigai_love: Watching a student's face light up when a concept finally clicks — and designing the systems that make that happen more consistently
profiles.ikigai_good_at: Curriculum design, learning science research, product roadmapping for K-12, and translating pedagogy into product decisions
profiles.ikigai_world_needs: Personalised learning paths that adapt to individual cognitive styles, not just pace, available in vernacular Indian languages
profiles.ikigai_paid_for: EdTech product consulting, curriculum audits, and learning experience design for schools and ed-platforms
profiles.ikigai_mission: To ensure every child in India has access to a teacher that truly understands them, through intelligent adaptive systems

match_criteria.collaboration_style: I bring the domain depth and product vision; I need a technical partner who respects pedagogy-driven design decisions and is comfortable with long feedback loops from teachers and students.
match_criteria.deal_breakers: Building EdTech as a pure technology problem without genuine respect for teachers as co-creators, or chasing BYJU's-style growth metrics
match_criteria.summary: Ex-teacher and EdTech PM with deep K-12 domain knowledge. Seeking a technical co-founder to build vernacular adaptive learning. Curriculum IP and pilot school partnerships already in place.

profiles.name: Vikram Singh
profiles.bio: Solidity developer and DeFi researcher. Built smart contract infrastructure at a Polygon-backed startup. Deeply interested in decentralised governance, on-chain identity, and how Web3 can reshape ownership for Indian creators.
profiles.ikigai_love: The philosophical and technical elegance of trustless systems — code that enforces agreements without requiring anyone to be trusted
profiles.ikigai_good_at: Solidity smart contract development, DeFi protocol design, on-chain data analysis, and zero-knowledge proof implementations
profiles.ikigai_world_needs: Decentralised financial rails that give artists, farmers, and small businesses genuine ownership over their economic relationships
profiles.ikigai_paid_for: Smart contract audits, DeFi protocol development, and Web3 technical advisory for early-stage projects
profiles.ikigai_mission: To build the ownership layer for India's creator and informal economy using verifiable, programmable infrastructure

match_criteria.collaboration_style: Fully async, documentation-heavy collaboration. I write extensive technical specs and expect the same rigour from collaborators. Prefer working with people who have genuine conviction in decentralisation.
match_criteria.deal_breakers: Projects that use blockchain as a buzzword without genuine decentralisation need, or teams building purely speculative token projects
match_criteria.summary: DeFi engineer with smart contract and ZK expertise. Seeking a co-founder with community, GTM, or tokenomics background to build decentralised ownership infrastructure for Indian creators.

profiles.name: Meera Patel
profiles.bio: Climate entrepreneur and environmental engineer. Built carbon measurement tools for MSME supply chains across Gujarat. Alumna of TERI School and IIM Ahmedabad. Believes the climate crisis is fundamentally a data and incentive problem.
profiles.ikigai_love: Finding elegant, market-based solutions to environmental problems that align business incentives with planetary health
profiles.ikigai_good_at: Carbon accounting methodologies, sustainability reporting (GRI/BRSR), supply chain emissions modelling, and stakeholder alignment in industrial settings
profiles.ikigai_world_needs: Affordable, accurate carbon measurement and trading infrastructure for Indian MSMEs who are excluded from global carbon markets
profiles.ikigai_paid_for: Sustainability consulting, carbon credit project development, ESG reporting for mid-size Indian manufacturers
profiles.ikigai_mission: To make Indian MSMEs active participants and beneficiaries in global carbon markets, not just compliance subjects

match_criteria.collaboration_style: I bring domain credibility, regulatory knowledge, and customer relationships. Need a technical co-founder who can move fast on an MVP and doesn't need hand-holding on architecture decisions.
match_criteria.deal_breakers: Greenwashing — building a product that looks sustainable but doesn't move the needle on actual emissions reduction
match_criteria.summary: Climate and sustainability expert with MSME market access across Gujarat. Building a carbon MRV and trading platform for Indian manufacturers. Seeking a technical co-founder to build the data and marketplace layer.

profiles.name: Siddharth Rao
profiles.bio: B2B SaaS operator who's taken two products from zero to ₹1Cr ARR. Background in sales engineering and customer success at Freshworks. Now building GTM infrastructure for vertical SaaS companies targeting SMB India.
profiles.ikigai_love: The craft of finding exactly the right customer, understanding their pain acutely, and building a repeatable motion to serve them
profiles.ikigai_good_at: Go-to-market strategy, sales process design, pricing and packaging for B2B SaaS, and building high-performance inside sales teams
profiles.ikigai_world_needs: Verticalized software built for Indian SMBs in their language, at their price point, with their workflow constraints in mind
profiles.ikigai_paid_for: GTM consulting, sales team setup and coaching, and fractional CRO for early-stage B2B SaaS startups
profiles.ikigai_mission: To help Indian software companies build sustainable, repeatable revenue engines that don't depend on blitzscaling or VC subsidies

match_criteria.collaboration_style: I own revenue and customer relationships end-to-end. Need a technical co-founder who can ship product fast, values customer feedback, and wants to understand why customers buy or churn.
match_criteria.deal_breakers: PLG-only founders who believe a great product sells itself and don't want to do manual sales, or anyone who underestimates the Indian SMB market
match_criteria.summary: Experienced B2B SaaS GTM operator seeking a technical co-founder. Have deep customer networks across manufacturing, retail, and logistics verticals. Ready to start with a laser-focused ICP.

profiles.name: Kavya Reddy
profiles.bio: Product designer and researcher. 5 years designing for fintech and consumer apps at Swiggy and Zepto. Deeply interested in the intersection of behavioural economics and interface design. Advocate for accessibility-first design systems.
profiles.ikigai_love: Creating interfaces that feel so natural and humane that users never have to think about the tool itself, only their intent
profiles.ikigai_good_at: End-to-end product design, design systems, user research facilitation, interaction design, and bridging design intent and engineering execution
profiles.ikigai_world_needs: Digital products designed for India's real users — diverse literacy levels, device constraints, languages, and behavioural patterns
profiles.ikigai_paid_for: Product design, design system creation, UX audits, and design leadership for Series A and Series B startups
profiles.ikigai_mission: To raise the bar for inclusive digital design in India so that every product works beautifully for every Indian, not just urban power users

match_criteria.collaboration_style: I work best in small product teams where design has a genuine seat at the table from day one, not as a polish layer at the end. I give and expect honest, direct design critique.
match_criteria.deal_breakers: Teams that treat design as making things pretty after engineering builds them, or founders who copy competitor UX without understanding their own users
match_criteria.summary: Senior product designer with deep fintech and consumer app experience. Available part-time for a founding team that values design as a core strategic function. Portfolio spans 0-to-1 and mature-stage products.

profiles.name: Aditya Kumar
profiles.bio: Data engineer and analytics architect. Built real-time data platforms processing 10M+ events/day at Flipkart and Meesho. Passionate about making data-driven decision-making accessible to operators, not just analysts.
profiles.ikigai_love: Building data infrastructure that quietly powers every business decision without anyone noticing it's there
profiles.ikigai_good_at: Designing event streaming architectures, building lakehouse data platforms, creating self-serve analytics layers, and optimising query performance at scale
profiles.ikigai_world_needs: Affordable, real-time data infrastructure for Indian startups that replaces gut-feel decisions with actionable, trustworthy signals
profiles.ikigai_paid_for: Data engineering, analytics architecture consulting, and data platform setup for growth-stage startups
profiles.ikigai_mission: To give every Indian startup — not just the well-funded ones — the data superpowers that only enterprise companies currently enjoy

match_criteria.collaboration_style: I like teams that are data-informed rather than data-paralyzed. Prefer async communication with clear written specs and weekly architecture reviews.
match_criteria.deal_breakers: Data silos, no monitoring culture, or teams that want dashboards without investing in data quality and governance
match_criteria.summary: Senior data engineer with real-time platform experience at Flipkart scale. Looking to join an early-stage team where I can architect the entire data stack from scratch and help the company become genuinely data-driven.

profiles.name: Divya Menon
profiles.bio: Consumer product builder and community obsessive. Previously PM at ShareChat and Meesho. Fascinated by how social dynamics and trust networks shape product adoption in vernacular India. Building a creator monetisation platform.
profiles.ikigai_love: Understanding how communities form, sustain, and evolve — and designing digital spaces that make those dynamics thrive
profiles.ikigai_good_at: Consumer product management, viral growth mechanics, community design, retention modelling, and live operations
profiles.ikigai_world_needs: Monetisation tools built for Indian vernacular creators who have massive engaged audiences but can't access western creator economy platforms
profiles.ikigai_paid_for: Consumer product strategy, growth consulting, and community management for Indian social and creator platforms
profiles.ikigai_mission: To help a million Indian creators earn their primary income from the communities they've already built

match_criteria.collaboration_style: High-energy, high-frequency collaboration. Daily check-ins, shared dashboards, strong opinions loosely held. I run tight product sprints and expect the team to move as fast as users demand.
match_criteria.deal_breakers: Building a consumer app without obsessive focus on retention and user delight from day one, or treating the Indian vernacular market as a secondary audience
match_criteria.summary: Ex-ShareChat consumer PM seeking a technical co-founder for a vernacular creator monetisation platform. Deep understanding of Bharat's creator landscape and strong early community relationships.

profiles.name: Rohit Bhatia
profiles.bio: AI product manager with experience deploying enterprise AI at HDFC Bank and Deloitte. Bridges the gap between what ML teams build and what business stakeholders actually need. Believer in responsible, explainable AI.
profiles.ikigai_love: Translating the messy complexity of machine learning into business value that non-technical leaders can trust and act on
profiles.ikigai_good_at: Enterprise AI product strategy, stakeholder alignment in large organisations, defining ML success metrics that map to business outcomes, and responsible AI frameworks
profiles.ikigai_world_needs: AI adoption frameworks and tooling that help traditional Indian enterprises — banking, insurance, manufacturing — deploy AI responsibly at scale
profiles.ikigai_paid_for: AI product management, enterprise AI strategy consulting, and AI governance advisory for regulated industries
profiles.ikigai_mission: To accelerate AI adoption in Indian enterprises by making AI systems more explainable, auditable, and trustworthy for the people who use and regulate them

match_criteria.collaboration_style: I navigate the enterprise sales and stakeholder landscape; I need a technical co-founder who can productise ML models and manage enterprise-grade reliability and compliance requirements.
match_criteria.deal_breakers: Building AI products without a clear answer to explainability and auditability for regulated industries, or underestimating enterprise sales cycles
match_criteria.summary: Enterprise AI product leader with HDFC and Deloitte experience. Available part-time to co-found an AI-native B2B product targeting Indian BFSI or manufacturing. Strong existing C-suite relationships.

profiles.name: Sneha Joshi
profiles.bio: Social entrepreneur and civic tech builder. Founded a digital literacy programme that trained 8,000 women in rural Maharashtra. Now using technology to make government services more accessible to first-time internet users.
profiles.ikigai_love: Using technology as a lever for dignity — ensuring marginalised communities can access the same services, information, and opportunities as anyone else
profiles.ikigai_good_at: Programme design, grassroots community mobilisation, government partnership navigation, and impact measurement frameworks
profiles.ikigai_world_needs: Simple, vernacular, offline-capable digital tools that help first-generation smartphone users access government welfare schemes and civic services
profiles.ikigai_paid_for: Social impact consulting, CSR programme design, and technology adoption strategy for NGOs and government bodies
profiles.ikigai_mission: To make digital India genuinely inclusive by designing government and civic services that work for users with low digital literacy

match_criteria.collaboration_style: Mission alignment is non-negotiable. I work weekends only and need asynchronous, documentation-heavy collaboration. I bring ground-level insight and institutional trust; I need technical depth.
match_criteria.deal_breakers: Treating social impact as a checkbox for funding rather than the core product value, or building without involving the communities being served in the design process
match_criteria.summary: Civic tech practitioner with grassroots community access across rural Maharashtra. Seeking a technical teammate for a vernacular digital services platform. NGO partnerships and pilot users already in place.

profiles.name: Karan Mehta
profiles.bio: Fintech founder who built and exited a B2B invoice financing platform. Now exploring embedded finance and BNPL infrastructure for India's 60 million kirana stores. IIM Calcutta MBA, ex-McKinsey financial services.
profiles.ikigai_love: Unlocking working capital for small merchants who've been locked out of formal credit by legacy underwriting models that don't understand their cash flow
profiles.ikigai_good_at: Financial product structuring, credit underwriting models, fintech regulatory navigation (RBI, NBFC), fundraising, and investor relations
profiles.ikigai_world_needs: Embedded credit products built into the workflows small merchants already use — their POS, their accounting app, their distributor portal
profiles.ikigai_paid_for: Fintech product strategy, credit model design, financial services regulatory consulting, and angel investing in early fintech
profiles.ikigai_mission: To make formal credit as natural and frictionless for a kirana store owner as it already is for a salaried professional

match_criteria.collaboration_style: I own finance, product strategy, and fundraising. Need a technical co-founder who can build robust, compliant financial infrastructure and has experience with account aggregator and OCEN integrations.
match_criteria.deal_breakers: Anyone who thinks embedded finance is just a distribution play and doesn't appreciate the credit risk and regulatory complexity underneath
match_criteria.summary: Second-time fintech founder with successful exit. Seeking a CTO co-founder to build embedded BNPL infrastructure for kiranas. Term sheet interest from two fintech-focused VCs already in hand.

profiles.name: Pooja Iyer
profiles.bio: UX designer specialising in healthtech and accessibility. Designed patient journeys for Apollo Hospitals digital and Practo. Passionate about making medical interfaces usable for patients across age groups and digital literacy levels.
profiles.ikigai_love: Designing healthcare experiences where patients feel informed, respected, and in control — not bewildered and anxious
profiles.ikigai_good_at: Patient journey mapping, accessibility-first UI design, clinical UX research, designing for low-literacy users, and healthcare service design
profiles.ikigai_world_needs: Healthtech products designed for patients as they actually are — older adults, low-digital-literacy users, people in distress — not for urban tech-savvy users
profiles.ikigai_paid_for: Healthcare UX consulting, clinical interface design, accessibility audits, and design research for healthtech startups
profiles.ikigai_mission: To make every digital healthcare touchpoint — from booking an appointment to understanding a diagnosis — feel humane and clear for every Indian patient

match_criteria.collaboration_style: I need to be embedded in product and engineering conversations from the earliest stage. I work best when research insights drive decisions, not just visual aesthetics. Async-first with structured design reviews.
match_criteria.deal_breakers: Healthtech teams that treat UX as a final visual pass rather than a core clinical safety consideration
match_criteria.summary: Specialist healthcare UX designer with Apollo and Practo experience. Available part-time for a healthtech startup that takes accessible, inclusive design seriously. Portfolio includes ICU dashboards and patient self-service portals.

profiles.name: Nikhil Agarwal
profiles.bio: ML engineer with a focus on computer vision and edge AI. IIT Kharagpur graduate. Built real-time defect detection systems for Tata Steel and crop disease identification for agritech. Now working on vision-language models for Indian industrial settings.
profiles.ikigai_love: Training neural networks to perceive the physical world and act on it reliably — the moment a model catches something a human would have missed
profiles.ikigai_good_at: Computer vision model development, edge AI deployment on constrained hardware, MLOps, and building feedback loops for continuous model improvement in production
profiles.ikigai_world_needs: Vision AI systems that work in real Indian industrial and agricultural settings — variable lighting, dusty cameras, unstable connectivity — not just benchmark datasets
profiles.ikigai_paid_for: Computer vision consulting, edge ML deployment, and AI R&D for manufacturing, agritech, and logistics companies
profiles.ikigai_mission: To make AI-powered quality control and visual inspection accessible to mid-size Indian manufacturers who currently rely entirely on manual inspection

match_criteria.collaboration_style: I move fast on research and prototyping. I need a business co-founder or operator who understands the factory floor or farm context and can translate my technical output into a product customers will pay for.
match_criteria.deal_breakers: Teams wanting to apply computer vision without domain expertise in the target industry, or who think a 95% accurate model is good enough for safety-critical inspection
match_criteria.summary: IIT Kharagpur ML engineer with production computer vision deployments in steel manufacturing and agriculture. Seeking a domain-expert co-founder or PM to commercialise edge vision AI for Indian industry.

profiles.name: Tanvi Sharma
profiles.bio: EdTech curriculum designer and teacher trainer. Built vernacular STEM curriculum used by 50,000 students across UP and Rajasthan through Teach for India. Now designing AI-powered tools to help overworked government school teachers personalise instruction.
profiles.ikigai_love: Empowering teachers — not replacing them — with tools that give them time and insight to focus on the students who need them most
profiles.ikigai_good_at: Curriculum design for multilingual settings, teacher professional development, learning outcome assessment frameworks, and stakeholder engagement with government education systems
profiles.ikigai_world_needs: Practical AI tools designed for government school teachers — usable on basic smartphones, available in Hindi and regional languages, requiring minimal training
profiles.ikigai_paid_for: Curriculum design consulting, teacher training programme delivery, and learning design advisory for EdTech platforms and government education bodies
profiles.ikigai_mission: To make every government school teacher in India feel supported, effective, and professionally valued through intelligent, contextually appropriate tools

match_criteria.collaboration_style: I need a technical co-founder who respects the complexity of government school systems and is willing to co-design with teachers, not just for them. Patient, mission-driven, strong on localisation.
match_criteria.deal_breakers: Building EdTech that treats teachers as the problem rather than the solution, or anyone chasing urban private school market scale while ignoring the government sector
match_criteria.summary: Teach for India curriculum designer with deep government school relationships in UP and Rajasthan. Seeking a technical co-founder to build Hindi-first teacher support tools. State education department interest already established.

profiles.name: Manish Verma
profiles.bio: Cloud infrastructure and platform engineering lead. Built multi-tenant SaaS infrastructure for 200+ enterprise customers at Zoho and Darwinbox. Expert in cost-optimised, secure AWS and GCP architectures. Loves developer experience almost as much as uptime.
profiles.ikigai_love: Engineering systems so reliable and self-healing that the teams depending on them can forget infrastructure exists and focus entirely on building product
profiles.ikigai_good_at: Cloud architecture (AWS/GCP), Kubernetes platform engineering, infrastructure-as-code, cost optimisation, SRE practices, and security compliance for multi-tenant SaaS
profiles.ikigai_world_needs: Opinionated, production-ready cloud infrastructure templates for Indian SaaS startups that don't have the resources to hire a dedicated platform team
profiles.ikigai_paid_for: Cloud architecture consulting, DevOps setup and automation, infrastructure cost optimisation, and fractional CTO/VP Engineering for early-stage startups
profiles.ikigai_mission: To eliminate infrastructure anxiety for Indian SaaS founders so they can focus on their product and customers, not uptime dashboards

match_criteria.collaboration_style: I build the foundation so others can move fast. I work best in async environments with strong documentation culture, clear on-call responsibilities, and founders who understand the value of platform investment.
match_criteria.deal_breakers: Founders who want to skip security and compliance because it's 'too early', or teams with no incident response culture
match_criteria.summary: Platform engineer with multi-tenant SaaS and enterprise compliance experience at Zoho and Darwinbox. Can architect and operate the entire cloud infrastructure layer for an early-stage B2B SaaS product.

profiles.name: Ritu Chawla
profiles.bio: D2C brand builder and growth operator. Scaled two consumer brands to ₹10Cr+ ARR on Shopify and quick-commerce. Deep expertise in performance marketing, brand storytelling, and unit economics for Indian D2C. Based in Delhi.
profiles.ikigai_love: Building brands that stand for something real and connecting them to customers who genuinely care — where every purchase is a small act of alignment with a value
profiles.ikigai_good_at: Performance marketing (Meta/Google), brand strategy, supply chain coordination, D2C unit economics modelling, and building repeat-purchase retention loops
profiles.ikigai_world_needs: Honest, well-crafted consumer brands built around Indian quality traditions — Ayurveda, handloom, regional food — that can compete on quality, not just price
profiles.ikigai_paid_for: D2C growth consulting, brand strategy, performance marketing management, and e-commerce operations for consumer brands
profiles.ikigai_mission: To help Indian artisans and traditional manufacturers build direct consumer relationships and earn fair value for world-class craft

match_criteria.collaboration_style: I move fast on brand and growth. I need a technical co-founder who can build our e-commerce tech stack and supply chain automation, and who is excited about building a brand that genuinely cares about artisans.
match_criteria.deal_breakers: Founders who treat brand as logo design rather than the entire customer experience, or who want to build a D2C brand without a genuine supply chain and quality story
match_criteria.summary: D2C growth operator with two successful ₹10Cr+ brand exits. Seeking a technical co-founder to build the platform layer for an artisan-to-consumer brand. Strong supplier relationships in UP and Rajasthan textiles.

profiles.name: Arjun Nambiar
profiles.bio: Embedded systems and IoT engineer from Trivandrum. Built smart agriculture sensors for ISRO spin-off and industrial IoT systems for Kerala's cashew processing industry. Passionate about solving physical-world problems with connected hardware.
profiles.ikigai_love: The moment when a physical sensor correctly reports a real-world condition for the first time — the bridge between the digital and physical becoming real
profiles.ikigai_good_at: Embedded firmware development, low-power IoT design, LPWAN protocol implementation, hardware-software co-design, and sensor fusion for industrial environments
profiles.ikigai_world_needs: Affordable, ruggedised IoT systems designed for Indian agricultural and industrial conditions — monsoon humidity, power fluctuations, intermittent connectivity
profiles.ikigai_paid_for: Embedded systems consulting, IoT prototype development, firmware engineering for hardware startups, and technical due diligence for deep-tech investors
profiles.ikigai_mission: To put intelligent sensing infrastructure into the hands of Indian farmers and small manufacturers who can't afford enterprise industrial IoT platforms

match_criteria.collaboration_style: I design and build the hardware and firmware; I need a co-founder who can navigate the business complexity of hardware sales cycles, distribution, and after-sales support in Indian agriculture.
match_criteria.deal_breakers: Pure software people who underestimate hardware complexity and timelines, or anyone who wants to do hardware without understanding the bill of materials and supply chain implications
match_criteria.summary: Embedded systems engineer with field-tested IoT deployments in Indian agriculture and industrial settings. Seeking a business-oriented co-founder for an affordable precision farming platform. Prototype ready for pilot.

profiles.name: Shruti Desai
profiles.bio: Growth and marketing technologist with expertise in data-driven acquisition, lifecycle automation, and attribution modelling. Previously Head of Growth at two Mumbai-based Series A startups. Builds growth systems, not just campaigns.
profiles.ikigai_love: Finding the precise combination of message, channel, timing, and audience that makes someone say 'this is exactly what I needed' at exactly the right moment
profiles.ikigai_good_at: Full-funnel growth strategy, marketing attribution modelling, CRM and lifecycle automation, SEO at scale, and building growth teams from zero
profiles.ikigai_world_needs: Growth infrastructure and playbooks designed for Indian startups that can't afford global martech stacks but need the same analytical rigour and personalisation capabilities
profiles.ikigai_paid_for: Fractional Head of Growth, growth strategy consulting, martech stack setup, and performance marketing for early-stage consumer and B2B startups
profiles.ikigai_mission: To help Indian startups build sustainable growth engines rooted in genuine user value, not paid acquisition treadmills

match_criteria.collaboration_style: I'm a multiplier for technical teams who've built something good but don't know how to grow it. I work part-time with 2-3 founding teams and bring full strategic attention to each. Weekly syncs, data-first decisions.
match_criteria.deal_breakers: Founders who want growth shortcuts without product-market fit, or who expect paid acquisition to substitute for genuine retention
match_criteria.summary: Fractional Head of Growth with full-funnel expertise across consumer and B2B. Currently available for 2 startups. Brings growth system design, attribution infrastructure, and lifecycle automation — not just campaign management.

messages.content: Hey Rohit — your profile stood out immediately. The combination of HDFC Bank and Deloitte enterprise AI experience is exactly the complement I've been looking for. I'm building an LLM-powered code review and documentation tool — the model pipeline is live with 3 beta customers (mid-size SaaS engineering teams) and we're seeing roughly 50% reduction in PR review cycle time. The part I haven't figured out is enterprise GTM and navigating procurement and compliance hurdles for larger customers. Would you be open to a co-founder conversation?

Arjun, this is genuinely interesting — a few questions before we go further. Who are the 3 beta customers and what's the primary use case: code review, documentation, or broader? What does expansion look like: single team land-and-expand or org-wide from day one? And what does your explainability story look like? That was the single biggest blocker for every AI tool we evaluated at HDFC — risk and compliance needed to understand why the model flagged something, not just that it did. That's the gap that kills enterprise AI deals at procurement.

Good questions — I'll be direct. Beta customers are a 30-person fintech startup, an 80-person B2B SaaS company, and a consultancy. Primary use cases are code review and PR summaries; docs are secondary. Land-and-expand with single squads is the model. On explainability: I have to be honest, I've thought about it but haven't built it properly. The model surfaces citations from the codebase but there's no structured audit log. You've just identified a gap I was vaguely aware of but hadn't prioritised. That might have been a mistake.

The explainability gap is fixable and honestly it's a moat — smaller competitors won't build it. The pattern that works is structured decision logs tied to a specific rule or risk category, exportable as a compliance report PDF. It sounds like overhead but it's what converts a 'never' from a BFSI risk team into a 'send us the SOC2 and we'll revisit.' Your fintech beta customer is the right wedge for exactly this reason. I think we should get on a call — when works for you this week? I want to share a GTM framework I've been developing for enterprise developer tooling.

Friday 9am works. Before we do — I want to be clear about what I'm looking for: a genuine co-founder, not an advisor. I can offer meaningful equity and a seat at the table on product direction from day one. If that's not where you are, I understand and we can still have a useful conversation. I'll share a 15-minute Loom of the current product before the call so we don't waste time on basics, and a product brief. Looking forward to it.

I appreciate the directness — I'm in the same headspace. I've been part-time at Deloitte for the last 4 months specifically to create space for this kind of conversation, so the co-founder discussion is very much on the table. Send me the Loom and the product brief. I'll come to the call having thought through a GTM framework for developer tooling in the Indian enterprise context — I have hypotheses I want to pressure-test with you. Friday 9am confirmed.

Loom demo: loom.com/share/arjun-devtool-demo — fair warning, the UI is rough, I've been heads-down on model quality. The product brief is on Notion: notion.so/arjunsharma/devtool-brief. The core capability is solid even if the surface isn't. See you Friday.

That was a genuinely productive 90 minutes — I left excited, which doesn't happen often. To confirm what I think we agreed: I'll spend 2 weeks doing structured discovery with 5 engineering contacts at mid-size tech companies and 2 BFSI contacts, focusing on the AI code review pain and the compliance objections. You'll build the audit log feature in parallel. We reconvene December 8th to compare notes, and if we're both still feeling good we formalise the co-founder conversation. Does that capture it?

That's exactly it. Audit log is done — pushed to main last night. It generates structured JSON logs per review with rule references and a confidence score, and there's a basic export-to-PDF for compliance reports. Rough around the edges but functional — you can see it at github.com/arjunsharma-ai/devtool (audit_trail branch is now merged to main). How's the discovery going? I've been pulling data on PR review volume at mid-size fintech firms and the numbers are compelling for the wedge thesis.

Discovery complete. 4 of 5 tech contacts see strong value — the consistent theme was 'we'd use this if it wasn't a black box,' and every single one mentioned compliance unprompted, which validates your decision to build the audit log. Both BFSI contacts were cautious but not closed — one said explicitly 'get me a SOC2 report and I can take this to procurement.' I think the product direction is right and the market is real. I'd like to formalise this: can you send me a cap table overview so we can have an informed conversation about co-founder structure? I'm ready to make this official.

Hi Pooja — I came across your portfolio and the patient self-service portal work you did for Apollo immediately caught my attention. I'm building a remote diagnostics platform for Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities — the context is different from Apollo, but the core UX challenge is similar: patients who aren't used to digital interfaces, often anxious, and being guided by an ASHA worker rather than using the product independently. Would love to understand if this kind of work interests you.

Priya, this is exactly the kind of problem I look for — the Apollo work was good but the patients were relatively urban and digitally literate, which is honestly the easier challenge. A few questions to understand the context: What devices are ASHA workers using and at what price range? Is the patient the primary user or always ASHA-assisted? And what's the connectivity assumption — fully offline, intermittent, or mostly connected with occasional drops?

ASHA workers have government-issued Android phones, mostly ₹6,000–10,000 range, Android 10 or 11. Always ASHA-assisted — patients are 35–65 years old, mixed literacy, many who've never touched a smartphone. Connectivity is intermittent so we're building offline-first with sync. Core ASHA flow: enrol a patient, capture vitals from a Bluetooth glucometer and BP cuff, answer a symptom questionnaire, submit for remote doctor review. I'm attaching a rough wireframe our engineer built — I believe it's too complex but I can't articulate exactly why.

I've looked at the wireframe — you're right, and I can articulate why. The navigation is 4 levels deep in places, the error states are text-only, and critically, it's been designed as a data entry form rather than a clinical workflow. Those are very different mental models. For ASHA workers in the field I'd advocate for a strictly linear step-by-step flow driven by illustrations and audio prompts with no visible branching logic. I did something similar for a cardiac rehab programme at Apollo where the primary users were older adults with low digital literacy — the cognitive load reduction was significant and measurable in task completion rates.

This is exactly the kind of feedback I couldn't get anywhere else — precise and immediately actionable. I'm attaching field research notes from 3 clinic visits in Sitapur, UP, including ASHA worker interviews and patient observation sessions that cover the physical context and specific moments where our prototype broke down. The question I haven't resolved at all is consent and privacy UX: how do you design meaningful, legally defensible informed consent for a patient with low literacy and no prior experience with digital health products?

The consent question is genuinely hard and underaddressed in Indian health UX. The pattern I'd advocate for: a three-layer approach — a 30-second illustrated visual strip explaining what data is collected and why; a verbal confirmation recorded as a short audio clip attached to the patient record; and a thumbprint-based acknowledgement on-screen. This satisfies ICMR guidelines on digital consent for low-literacy populations while being accessible in practice. I'd like to propose a 2-week design discovery sprint: I'd deliver journey maps for the 3 core ASHA workflows, a tested prototype for 2 key flows, and a research synthesis document. Would that be useful?

That would be enormously useful. Can you tell me more about how you structure the engagement — time commitment per week, what you'd need from me, and how you think about compensation? I want to be upfront: I'm pre-revenue with a small grant budget, so I need to be thoughtful about cash, but I also don't want to undervalue what you bring.

I work with early-stage founders regularly and have two models. Option A: a paid discovery engagement at ₹80,000 for the 2 weeks, roughly 15 hours total, no ongoing commitment. Option B: a smaller upfront payment of ₹20,000 plus 0.5–0.75% equity, with the expectation we're exploring a longer-term design partnership — in that case I'd also want to visit one of your pilot sites, because I won't design for a population I haven't observed in context. Time commitment from you is about 8 hours across the 2 weeks for co-working sessions and feedback rounds.

Option B feels right for both of us — I want a design partner invested in the outcome. On equity, I'm comfortable at 0.75% given the scope I have in mind beyond just this sprint. The pilot site visit is a great idea; I'm heading to Sitapur the last week of November and you'd be welcome to join. Accommodation and travel are covered. Can you share your standard NDA and engagement terms so we can formalise this?

I'm genuinely excited about this — the Sitapur visit will make everything better. I'm attaching my standard NDA and a short engagement framework document (2 pages). Once we've both signed, I'll send a kick-off questionnaire to gather context before we start. Let's schedule a 45-minute kick-off call next week — I want to understand the pilot sites, the current ASHA training programme, and any existing artefacts like journey maps or prior user research. Looking forward to working together, Priya.

Hi Rahul — I noticed your experience with UPI AutoPay mandates and Kafka-based payment processing at PhonePe, which is directly relevant to what I'm building. The product is embedded BNPL for kirana stores: merchants use a credit line to pay their distributors, not to take cash out. The technical crux is account aggregator integration for cash flow underwriting and a reliable UPI AutoPay repayment mechanism. I've hit walls on both. Would you be open to a technical conversation?

Karan, the product design is smarter than most BNPL I've seen — integrating credit into the distributor payment flow rather than giving merchants cash is the right call for credit risk. Specific technical questions: Which AA provider, and is the consent artefact flow integrated? For credit decisioning, are you using AA cash flow as the primary signal or supplementing with GST or ONDC purchase data? And on repayment — are you using UPI AutoPay mandates? The mandate failure rate on low-balance merchant accounts is a problem I've spent a lot of time on.

We're on Setu for AA — consent flow is integrated, pulling 12-month bank statement data. Credit decisioning is rule-based right now (monthly inflow threshold plus volatility score), ML scoring is on the roadmap. On repayment: yes, we planned UPI AutoPay mandates — I hadn't specifically modelled the low-balance failure rate. Can you elaborate on what you've seen? I'm attaching a one-page architecture sketch of the current system. If the AutoPay failure rate is higher than I've assumed it would materially change our unit economics.

AutoPay mandate failure on small merchant accounts runs 25–35% in my experience — the core issue is timing. Kiranas pay distributors in cash on Mondays and their UPI balance is often near-zero by Thursday when mandates typically execute. The fix: try AutoPay first, fall back to a UPI collect request with a 48-hour window, escalate to a manual call only after that. It adds complexity but it's recoverable. Your architecture sketch looks reasonable overall. One critical question: what's your NBFC tie-up status? That determines your collections authority and what you can own versus what you're dependent on a partner for.

The AutoPay timing analysis is valuable — we'd modelled a flat 5% failure rate, 25–35% changes our collections cost significantly. NBFC tie-up is signed with Avanse Financial, non-exclusive, good terms. OCEN 4.0 integration is in progress for loan origination. Current team is me and one junior backend engineer — good, but not senior enough to own architecture decisions alone. I want to ask you directly: is this something you'd consider as a co-founding role? I'm offering the CTO position. I know you're at PhonePe — I want to understand your timeline and what would make this worth the leap.

I appreciate the directness so I'll match it. I'm genuinely interested — the product design is smarter than most fintech I've reviewed, you have the NBFC in place, and OCEN is the right integration choice. My constraints: I'm on a 3-month notice period at PhonePe and I won't break it. On equity — I need to understand the structure before going further. I have 6 years of deep payments infrastructure experience and I won't take a token technical co-founder slice. What are you thinking for CTO equity and vesting, and what's the current funding status?

CTO equity: 15–18%, 4-year vest with 1-year cliff — I'm not going to low-ball you. On funding: I'm in early conversations with QED and Stellaris, no term sheet yet but strong signal, which means your 3-month notice actually aligns reasonably well with when we'd need you full-time. Practical proposal: a paid consulting engagement starting now at ₹1.5L/month so we can work together on the architecture before you've committed to anything formally. It gives both sides a chance to validate the working relationship before signing.

The equity range is workable and the consulting-first approach is the right way to de-risk this for both of us. Here's how I'd structure it: I'll do a full technical architecture review focused on the AA integration, credit decisioning data model, and repayment flow. I'll produce a written document with findings and recommendations. That gives you something concrete to evaluate my thinking, and gives me enough depth to know what I'd be inheriting. Two weeks, then we compare notes and decide. Does that work?

That works perfectly and it's the kind of rigour I'm looking for. Sharing access to our private GitHub repo, the Setu integration docs, Avanse API documentation, and our current data model. There's also a Notion doc with our credit policy logic. One thing I'll flag: the repayment ledger was built by our junior engineer and I suspect it has scaling issues, but I don't have the depth to diagnose it properly. Take whatever time you need — I'd rather have an honest assessment than a fast one.

Architecture review complete. Three critical issues: (1) Race condition between the loan origination event and NBFC notification — under concurrent load this will cause double-disbursements, I've written a fix. (2) The AA consent fetch has no idempotency key, so retry logic on network failure can trigger duplicate consent requests that will get flagged by Setu compliance. (3) The repayment ledger uses a mutable running balance column rather than an immutable event log — this will corrupt under any concurrent repayment scenario and won't survive past roughly 10,000 merchants without a full rewrite. I've documented solutions for all three. Despite this, the overall architecture is sound — the problems are fixable in 2–3 weeks. I'm ready to start the paid engagement. Let's talk tomorrow.

Hi Manish — your Zoho and Darwinbox infrastructure experience is exactly what caught my eye. I'm building a vertical SaaS for Indian textile manufacturers — compliance documentation, export certification workflow, and supplier management. GTM is my strength (I have 8 warm prospects in Surat and Tirupur), but I need a technical co-founder who's built multi-tenant SaaS before and won't need to learn the hard lessons from scratch. Would you be interested in exploring this?

The textile manufacturing vertical is interesting — the infrastructure requirements map well to what I've built. A few questions: SaaS or will customers ask for on-premise deployment? Some mid-size manufacturers are very wary of putting supplier pricing in the cloud. What's the data sensitivity level? And do you genuinely need multi-tenancy from day one or can you start with simpler per-customer deployments and migrate later?

SaaS — I've talked to 6 of the 8 prospects and on-premise came up once from the most conservative one. Data sensitivity is medium: compliance certifications and buyer-supplier pricing are commercially sensitive but we're not handling financial transactions or personal data. Multi-tenancy from day one is a genuine requirement — two prospects specifically asked whether their supplier lists would be isolated from competitors' views. They're sophisticated enough to ask the right question. How do you handle the on-premise objection in practice without building two separate products?

The on-premise objection is almost always a proxy for a trust question, not a genuine infrastructure requirement. At Darwinbox we handled it with a dedicated VPC deployment option — the customer gets their own isolated AWS environment, we manage it, data never shares compute with other tenants. Adds ₹40–60k/month to infrastructure cost but closes the deal. On multi-tenancy architecture: for your scale I'd actually recommend schema-based multi-tenancy in PostgreSQL to start rather than anything more complex. It gives genuine data isolation for compliance purposes and you can migrate to a more sophisticated pattern when you actually need to. I used this at Zoho for 3 years before we outgrew it.

The dedicated VPC framing makes complete sense — I'll use that with the conservative prospect immediately. The schema-based multi-tenancy approach is exactly what I needed to hear; I was worried we'd need something complex from day one. Here's a question that's become urgent: my best prospect, a ₹400 Cr Surat exporter, asked about security certifications in our last call and specifically mentioned SOC2. We're pre-product. What's the realistic path to having something credible to show an enterprise security team?

SOC2 is manageable if you start correctly. Type 1 (point-in-time) takes 10–12 weeks with tooling like Vanta or Drata — cost is roughly ₹4–6L for tooling and the auditor combined. Type 2 requires a 6-month observation period, which most Indian SMB deals don't require upfront. Practical answer for your Surat prospect: send them a completed security questionnaire and a letter stating you're actively pursuing SOC2 Type 1 with a target date. That unblocks most deals. I'm attaching a security posture template I use with enterprise prospects — it covers the questions Indian manufacturing procurement teams actually ask.

This is exactly the clarity I needed — I'll send the security questionnaire to the Surat prospect this week. I want to move beyond the exploratory conversation. Are you free to meet in Hyderabad for a half-day working session? I'd like to walk you through the full product vision and all 8 customer conversations in person — I think the picture is clearer in that format. And I want to understand how you think about the build sequence before we talk about co-founder structure.

Yes — week of December 16th works, I'm based in Hyderabad. Send me the product spec and any customer discovery notes in advance so we can focus the working session on architecture and decisions rather than introductions. Also: are you thinking Next.js for the frontend or is the stack still open? I have strong opinions about the right choices for a B2B SaaS at this stage and I'd rather share them before we're locked into something.

That was the best 4 hours I've spent since starting this venture. Three decisions we made that I want to capture: (1) Tech stack — Next.js 14 with Postgres, deployed on AWS with Terraform-managed infrastructure from day one. (2) First 6 months — deploy manually for the first 3 customers, no automation until we understand the real operational complexity. (3) Equity conversation — we both agreed to start it formally in January once you've reviewed the full financials and cap table. I'm attaching the GTM doc I mentioned — 8 prospects, pipeline value, and the ICP definition. Can you put the infrastructure cost estimates from the whiteboard into a doc?

Infrastructure doc attached. Phase 1 (0–50 customers): under ₹45,000/month on AWS using RDS Postgres, ECS Fargate, S3, and CloudFront, all managed with Terraform. Phase 2 (50–500 customers): estimated ₹1.8–2.5L/month when we add read replicas and proper observability. I've also included a preliminary multi-tenancy schema design and a SOC2 Type 1 readiness checklist. I've been thinking about nothing else since our meeting. I'm ready to have the co-founder conversation — let's find time this week to go through the cap table and term structure.
